◆ session_render_loop()

asciichat_error_t session_render_loop ( session_capture_ctx_t * capture,
session_display_ctx_t * display,
session_should_exit_fn should_exit,
session_capture_fn capture_cb,
session_sleep_for_frame_fn sleep_cb,
session_keyboard_handler_fn keyboard_handler,
void * user_data )

#include <src/common/session/render.h>

Unified render loop for all display modes.

Flexible render loop that supports both synchronous and event-driven modes. Optionally integrates keyboard input handling for interactive controls.

Synchronous Mode (capture != NULL, callbacks == NULL): Handles complete render lifecycle:

  • Frame rate limiting via session_capture_sleep_for_fps()
  • Frame capture via session_capture_read_frame()
  • ASCII conversion via session_display_convert_to_ascii()
  • Terminal rendering via session_display_render_frame()
  • Keyboard input polling and handling (if handler provided)
  • Memory cleanup with SAFE_FREE()

Used by: mirror mode, discovery participant mode

Event-Driven Mode (capture == NULL, callbacks != NULL): Delegates all frame operations to callbacks:

  • Custom frame capture via capture callback (can return NULL)
  • Custom timing via sleep_for_frame callback
  • ASCII conversion via session_display_convert_to_ascii()
  • Terminal rendering via session_display_render_frame()
  • Keyboard input polling and handling (if handler provided)
  • Memory cleanup with SAFE_FREE()

Used by: client mode, async frame sources

Keyboard Support:

  • If keyboard_handler is provided, initializes keyboard input at loop start
  • Polls for keyboard input each frame iteration
  • Calls keyboard_handler callback when keys are pressed
  • Cleans up keyboard on loop exit
  • Safe to pass NULL for no keyboard support (backward compatible)
Parameters
captureCapture context for synchronous mode (NULL if using callbacks)
displayDisplay context (must not be NULL)
should_exitCallback to check exit condition each iteration (must not be NULL)
capture_cbCustom capture callback for event-driven mode (NULL if using capture context)
sleep_cbCustom sleep callback for event-driven mode (NULL if using capture context)
keyboard_handlerOptional keyboard handler callback (NULL for no keyboard support)
user_dataOpaque pointer passed to all callbacks
Returns
ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure
Note
Either capture XOR (capture_cb && sleep_cb) must be provided, not both
Caller must free capture and display contexts after this returns
In synchronous mode, capture callback can return NULL on frame unavailability
In event-driven mode, capture callback can return NULL gracefully
keyboard_handler is optional - pass NULL to disable keyboard support
Synchronous Example (Mirror Mode)
static bool my_should_exit(void *user_data) {
return atomic_load(&g_exit_flag);
}
int main(void) {
session_capture_ctx_t *capture = session_capture_create(NULL);
session_display_ctx_t *display = session_display_create(NULL);
platform_set_console_ctrl_handler(my_ctrl_handler);
asciichat_error_t result = session_render_loop(
capture, display, my_should_exit,
NULL, NULL, // No custom callbacks
NULL // No user_data needed
);
session_display_destroy(display);
session_capture_destroy(capture);
return result == ASCIICHAT_OK ? 0 : (int)result;
}
session_render_loop
asciichat_error_t session_render_loop(session_capture_ctx_t *capture, session_display_ctx_t *display, session_should_exit_fn should_exit, session_capture_fn capture_cb, session_sleep_for_frame_fn sleep_cb, session_keyboard_handler_fn keyboard_handler, void *user_data)
Unified render loop for all display modes.
Definition common/session/render.c:55
main
int main(int argc, char *argv[])
Definition main.c:442
Event-Driven Example (Client Mode)
static image_t *client_capture_frame(void *user_data) {
client_ctx_t *ctx = (client_ctx_t *)user_data;
return network_frame_queue_pop_nonblocking(ctx->frame_queue);
}
static void client_sleep_for_frame(void *user_data) {
client_ctx_t *ctx = (client_ctx_t *)user_data;
if (network_frame_queue_empty(ctx->frame_queue)) {
platform_sleep_ms(10);
}
}
static bool client_should_exit(void *user_data) {
return !server_connection_is_active();
}
int main(void) {
session_display_ctx_t *display = session_display_create(NULL);
client_ctx_t client_ctx = {...};
asciichat_error_t result = session_render_loop(
NULL, display, client_should_exit, // NULL capture = event-driven
client_capture_frame, // Custom capture
client_sleep_for_frame, // Custom sleep
&client_ctx // Context for callbacks
);
session_display_destroy(display);
return result == ASCIICHAT_OK ? 0 : (int)result;
}
platform_sleep_ms
void platform_sleep_ms(unsigned int ms)
Definition platform/wasm/time.c:24
server_connection_is_active
bool server_connection_is_active()
Check if server connection is currently active.
Definition src/client/server.c:774

Definition at line 55 of file common/session/render.c.

58 {
59 // Validate required parameters
60 log_info("[SESSION_RENDER_LOOP] START: capture=%p, display=%p, mode=%s",
61 (void *)capture, (void *)display, capture ? "SYNCHRONOUS" : "EVENT-DRIVEN");
62
63 if (!display) {
64 SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session_render_loop: display context is NULL");
65 return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
66 }
67
68 if (!should_exit) {
69 SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session_render_loop: should_exit callback is NULL");
70 return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
71 }
72
73 // Validate mode: either capture context OR custom callbacks, not both
74 if (!capture && !capture_cb) {
75 SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session_render_loop: must provide either capture context or capture callback");
76 return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
77 }
78
79 if (capture && capture_cb) {
80 SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session_render_loop: cannot provide both capture context and capture callback");
81 return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
82 }
83
84 // In event-driven mode, both capture_cb and sleep_cb must be provided together
85 if (capture_cb && !sleep_cb) {
86 SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session_render_loop: capture_cb requires sleep_cb (must provide both or neither)");
87 return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
88 }
89
90 if (sleep_cb && !capture_cb) {
91 SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session_render_loop: sleep_cb requires capture_cb (must provide both or neither)");
92 return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
93 }
94
95 // SYNCHRONOUS MODE: Use threaded pipeline for capture + render
96 if (capture != NULL) {
97 log_info("[SESSION_RENDER_LOOP_SYNCHRONOUS] Creating pipeline... (capture=%p, display=%p)", (void*)capture, (void*)display);
98 session_pipeline_t *pipeline = NULL;
99 asciichat_error_t pipeline_err = session_pipeline_create(capture, display, &pipeline);
100 log_info("[SESSION_RENDER_LOOP_SYNCHRONOUS] pipeline_create returned: %d (pipeline=%p)", pipeline_err, (void*)pipeline);
101 if (pipeline_err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
102 log_error("[SESSION_RENDER_LOOP_SYNCHRONOUS] Pipeline creation failed: %d", pipeline_err);
103 return pipeline_err;
104 }
105 log_info("[SESSION_RENDER_LOOP_SYNCHRONOUS] Pipeline created successfully, running main loop...");
106
107 asciichat_error_t run_err = session_pipeline_run_main(pipeline, should_exit, keyboard_handler, user_data);
108
109 // In snapshot mode, pass actual capture-based elapsed time to encoder for correct video duration
110 if (GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode) && display && g_snapshot_first_capture_ns > 0) {
111 uint64_t now_ns = time_get_ns();
112 uint64_t elapsed_ns = now_ns - g_snapshot_first_capture_ns;
113 double capture_elapsed_sec = (double)elapsed_ns / (double)NS_PER_SEC_INT;
114 log_info("SNAPSHOT: Pipeline finished - passing capture_elapsed=%.3f to encoder", capture_elapsed_sec);
115 session_display_set_snapshot_actual_duration(display, capture_elapsed_sec);
116 }
117
118 session_pipeline_destroy(pipeline);
119 return run_err;
120 } else {
121 log_warn("[SESSION_RENDER_LOOP] capture is NULL - will not use pipeline (capture_cb=%p)", (void*)capture_cb);
122 }
123
124 // Snapshot mode state tracking
125 bool snapshot_mode = GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode);
126 bool snapshot_done = false;
127 uint64_t frames_rendered_since_first = 0; // Track frames rendered after first frame (for snapshot delay)
128 // NOTE: g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered is set by display.c when first frame is rendered via platform_write_all()
129
130 // Help screen state tracking for clear-screen transition
131 bool help_was_active = false;
132
133 // Terminal resize tracking (for auto_width/auto_height mode)
134 unsigned short int last_terminal_width = terminal_get_effective_width();
135 unsigned short int last_terminal_height = terminal_get_effective_height();
136
137 log_info("session_render_loop: STARTING - display=%p capture=%p capture_cb=%p snapshot_mode=%s snapshot_delay=%.2f",
138 (void *)display, (void *)capture, (void *)capture_cb, snapshot_mode ? "YES" : "NO",
139 snapshot_mode ? GET_OPTION(snapshot_delay) : 0.0);
140 log_info("session_render_loop: About to enter main loop, should_exit fn check...");
141 if (should_exit(user_data)) {
142 log_warn("session_render_loop: EXIT signal already set at entry, aborting render loop");
143 return ASCIICHAT_OK;
144 }
145 log_info("session_render_loop: OK to proceed with main loop");
146
147
148 // Keyboard input initialization (if keyboard handler is provided)
149 // Disable keyboard only in snapshot mode
150 // Keyboard is pre-initialized from asciichat_shared_init()
151 // Allow keyboard input in all other modes if handler provided
152 bool keyboard_enabled = keyboard_handler && !snapshot_mode;
153
154 // Pause mode state tracking (for pause/unpause transitions in event-driven mode)
155 bool initial_paused_frame_rendered = false;
156 bool is_paused = false;
157 log_info("render_loop: Keyboard setup - handler=%p snapshot=%s enabled=%s", (void *)keyboard_handler,
158 snapshot_mode ? "YES" : "NO", keyboard_enabled ? "YES" : "NO");
159
160 // Log TTY detection status for debugging keyboard issues
161 log_info("TTY Status: stdin_tty=%d stdout_tty=%d interactive=%d keyboard_enabled=%d", terminal_is_stdin_tty(),
162 terminal_is_stdout_tty(), terminal_is_interactive(), keyboard_enabled);
163
164 // Frame rate timing (event-driven mode only)
165 uint64_t frame_count = 0;
166 uint64_t frame_start_ns = 0;
167
168 // Event-driven mode: disable console logging during rendering to prevent logs from corrupting frame display
169 // This must be done BEFORE the render loop to avoid repeated lock/unlock cycles that could cause deadlocks
170 log_info("[EVENT_DRIVEN_MODE] Entering render loop. snapshot_mode=%s", snapshot_mode ? "YES" : "NO");
171 log_set_terminal_output(false);
172
173 // Main render loop
174 log_debug("session_render_loop: entering main loop");
175 int loop_iteration = 0;
176 log_info("[RENDER_LOOP_START] snapshot_mode=%s, snapshot_delay=%.2f", snapshot_mode ? "YES" : "NO",
177 snapshot_mode ? GET_OPTION(snapshot_delay) : 0.0);
178
179 // Wait for splash animation to complete before rendering first frame
180 // This prevents ASCII art from mixing with splash screen output
181 static bool splash_wait_done = false;
182 if (!splash_wait_done) {
183 splash_wait_done = true;
184 // Re-enable logging briefly for splash wait, then disable again
185 log_set_terminal_output(true);
186 splash_wait_for_animation();
187 log_set_terminal_output(false);
188 }
189
190 // In snapshot mode, we must render at least one frame before checking should_exit
191 // to ensure the snapshot delay timer can start
192 bool force_first_iteration = snapshot_mode && frame_count == 0;
193
194 while (force_first_iteration || (!should_exit(user_data) && !(snapshot_mode && snapshot_done))) {
195 force_first_iteration = false;
196 loop_iteration++;
197 log_info("[LOOP_ITER] iteration=%d, snapshot_done=%s, frame_count=%lu", loop_iteration, snapshot_done ? "YES" : "NO", frame_count);
198 if (loop_iteration % 60 == 0) {
199 log_debug("session_render_loop: iteration %d, should_exit check returning false", loop_iteration);
200 }
201 // Snapshot mode: exit at start of iteration if done
202 // This prevents frame 2+ from being captured when snapshot_delay has elapsed
203 if (snapshot_mode && snapshot_done && frame_count > 0) {
204 log_info("[SNAPSHOT_EXIT] Snapshot mode: exiting at loop iteration start (snapshot_done=true, frames=%lu)", frame_count);
205 break;
206 }
207
208 log_debug_every(US_PER_SEC_INT, "session_render_loop: frame %lu", frame_count);
209 // Frame timing - measure total time to maintain target FPS
210 frame_start_ns = time_get_ns();
211
212 // Frame capture and timing - mode-dependent
213 image_t *image = NULL;
214 uint64_t capture_start_ns = 0;
215 uint64_t capture_end_ns = 0;
216 uint64_t pre_convert_ns = 0;
217 uint64_t post_convert_ns = 0;
218 uint64_t conversion_elapsed_ns = 0;
219
220 // EVENT-DRIVEN MODE: Frames come from callbacks
221 // Both sleep_cb and capture_cb are guaranteed non-NULL by validation above
222 sleep_cb(user_data);
223 image = capture_cb(user_data);
224
225 if (!image) {
226 // No frame available - this is normal in async modes (network latency, etc.)
227 // Just continue to next iteration, don't exit
228 continue;
229 }
230
231 // Event-driven mode: increment frame count
232 frame_count++;
233
234 // Check for terminal resize (if auto_width or auto_height is enabled)
235 // This allows the render to adapt immediately when the user resizes the terminal
236 bool auto_width = GET_OPTION(auto_width);
237 bool auto_height = GET_OPTION(auto_height);
238 if (auto_width || auto_height) {
239 unsigned short int current_width = 0;
240 unsigned short int current_height = 0;
241
242 asciichat_error_t size_err = get_terminal_size(&current_width, &current_height);
243 if (size_err == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
244 bool width_changed = auto_width && (current_width != last_terminal_width);
245 bool height_changed = auto_height && (current_height != last_terminal_height);
246
247 if (width_changed || height_changed) {
248 if (width_changed) {
249 options_set_int("width", current_width);
250 log_info("Terminal width changed: %u → %u", last_terminal_width, current_width);
251 last_terminal_width = current_width;
252 }
253 if (height_changed) {
254 options_set_int("height", current_height);
255 log_info("Terminal height changed: %u → %u", last_terminal_height, current_height);
256 last_terminal_height = current_height;
257 }
258 // Clear screen when terminal is resized to avoid visual artifacts
259 terminal_clear_screen();
260 }
261 }
262 }
263
264 // Convert image to ASCII using display context
265 // Handles all palette, terminal caps, width, height, stretch settings
266 pre_convert_ns = time_get_ns();
267 char *ascii_frame = session_display_convert_to_ascii(display, image);
268 post_convert_ns = time_get_ns();
269 conversion_elapsed_ns = post_convert_ns - pre_convert_ns;
270
271 // DEBUG: Log frame conversion to file
272 FILE *debug_log = fopen("/tmp/render_loop_debug.log", "a");
273 if (debug_log) {
274 fprintf(debug_log, "[FRAME_CONVERT] frame %lu: image=%p, ascii_frame=%p (len=%zu), conversion_ms=%u\n",
275 frame_count, (void *)image, (void *)ascii_frame,
276 ascii_frame ? strlen(ascii_frame) : 0,
277 (unsigned)(conversion_elapsed_ns / NS_PER_MS_INT));
278 fclose(debug_log);
279 }
280
281 if (frame_count <= 3) {
282 log_info("RENDER_LOOP[%lu]: image=%p, ascii_frame=%p (conversion took %u ms)",
283 frame_count, (void *)image, (void *)ascii_frame,
284 (unsigned)(conversion_elapsed_ns / NS_PER_MS_INT));
285 }
286
287 // Declare variables that need scope beyond the if (ascii_frame) block
288 bool is_paused_frame = initial_paused_frame_rendered && is_paused;
289 bool output_paused_frame = snapshot_mode && is_paused_frame;
290 uint64_t pre_render_ns = 0, post_render_ns = 0;
291
292 if (ascii_frame) {
293 log_info_every(1 * NS_PER_SEC_INT, "render_loop: ascii_frame ready (len=%zu)", strlen(ascii_frame));
294
295 // Always attempt to render frames; the display context will handle filtering based on:
296 // - TTY mode: render all frames with cursor control (even in snapshot mode, for animation)
297 // - Piped mode: render all frames without cursor control (for continuous capture)
298 // - Snapshot mode on non-TTY: only the display context renders the final frame
299 // BUT: don't render if splash screen is still animating - wait for it to finish
300 bool splash_running = splash_is_running();
301 bool should_write = !splash_running;
302 if (frame_count == 1) {
303 log_dev("[RENDER_FRAME] Frame 1: splash_is_running=%s, should_write=%s", splash_running ? "YES" : "NO", should_write ? "YES" : "NO");
304 }
305 if (should_write) {
306 // is_final = true when: snapshot done, or paused frame (for both snapshot and pause modes)
307 // Profile: render frame
308 pre_render_ns = time_get_ns();
309 START_TIMER("render_frame");
310
311 if (frame_count <= 5 || frame_count % 10 == 0) {
312 log_info("RENDER_LOOP: Frame %lu - calling session_display_render_frame, display=%p",
313 (unsigned long)frame_count, (void *)display);
314 }
315
316 // Check if help screen is active - if so, render help instead of frame
317 // Help screen is disabled in snapshot mode and non-interactive terminals (keyboard disabled)
318 bool help_is_active = display && keyboard_help_is_active(display);
319
320 // Detect transition from help to ASCII art rendering
321 // When help closes, clear the screen before rendering ASCII art
322 if (help_was_active && !help_is_active) {
323 terminal_clear_screen();
324 log_debug_every(1 * NS_PER_SEC_INT, "Cleared screen when transitioning from help to ASCII art");
325 }
326
327 if (help_is_active) {
328 keyboard_help_render(display);
329 } else {
330 session_display_render_frame(display, ascii_frame);
331 }
332
333 // Snapshot mode: increment frame counter for all displayed frames
334 // Counter starts at 1 for the first displayed frame, then 2, 3, 4... for subsequent frames
335 // Timer is started by display.c when g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered is set
336 if (snapshot_mode && g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered) {
337 frames_rendered_since_first++;
338 log_info_every(1 * NS_PER_SEC_INT, "[SNAPSHOT] Frame rendered: frames_rendered_since_first=%lu", frames_rendered_since_first);
339 }
340
341 // Update help state for next iteration
342 help_was_active = help_is_active;
343
344 STOP_TIMER("render_frame");
345 }
346
347 // Keyboard input polling (if enabled) - MUST come before snapshot exit check so help screen can be toggled
348 // Allow keyboard in snapshot mode too (for help screen toggle debugging)
349 // Only enable keyboard if BOTH stdin AND stdout are TTYs to avoid buffering issues
350 // when tcsetattr() modifies the tty line discipline
351 if (keyboard_enabled && keyboard_handler) {
352 START_TIMER("keyboard_read_%lu", (unsigned long)frame_count);
353 keyboard_key_t key = keyboard_read_nonblocking();
354 double keyboard_elapsed_ns = STOP_TIMER("keyboard_read_%lu", (unsigned long)frame_count);
355 if (keyboard_elapsed_ns >= 0.0) {
356 char _duration_str[32];
357 time_pretty((uint64_t)keyboard_elapsed_ns, -1, _duration_str, sizeof(_duration_str));
358 log_dev_every(1 * NS_PER_SEC_INT, "RENDER[%lu] Keyboard read complete (key=%d) in %s",
359 (unsigned long)frame_count, key, _duration_str);
360 }
361 if (key != KEY_NONE) {
362 log_debug("KEYBOARD: Key pressed: code=%d char='%c'", key, (key >= 32 && key < 127) ? key : '?');
363 // Check if interactive grep should handle this key
364 if (log_search_should_handle(key)) {
365 log_debug("KEYBOARD: Grep handler taking key %d", key);
366 log_search_handle_key(key);
367 continue; // Force immediate re-render
368 }
369
370 // Normal keyboard handler
371 log_debug("KEYBOARD: Normal handler taking key %d", key);
372 keyboard_handler(capture, key, user_data);
373 }
374 }
375
376
377 // Free frame before checking exit conditions to avoid double-free
378 SAFE_FREE(ascii_frame);
379 }
380
381 // Snapshot mode: check if elapsed time has reached snapshot_delay duration (terminal render timing)
382 // Separate from capture timing - both should measure their own snapshot_delay
383 if (snapshot_mode && !snapshot_done) {
384 if (g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered && g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered_ns > 0) {
385 double snapshot_delay = GET_OPTION(snapshot_delay);
386 uint64_t now_ns = time_get_ns();
387 uint64_t elapsed_ns = now_ns - g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered_ns;
388 double elapsed_sec = (double)elapsed_ns / (double)NS_PER_SEC_INT;
389
390 if (frames_rendered_since_first == 1 || frames_rendered_since_first % 20 == 0) {
391 log_info("SNAPSHOT: RENDER_CHECK iter=%lu elapsed=%.3f target=%.2f (from first_frame_rendered)",
392 frames_rendered_since_first, elapsed_sec, snapshot_delay);
393 }
394
395 // snapshot_delay=0 means exit after first frame
396 // snapshot_delay>0 means wait that many seconds before exiting
397 // Exit is based on elapsed wall-clock time, not frame count
398 bool should_exit = (snapshot_delay == 0.0) || (elapsed_sec >= snapshot_delay);
399
400 if (should_exit) {
401 log_info("SNAPSHOT: RENDER_DONE at iteration %lu - elapsed=%.3f target=%.2f (setting snapshot_done=true)",
402 frames_rendered_since_first, elapsed_sec, snapshot_delay);
403 // We don't end frames with newlines so the next log would print on the same line as the frame's
404 // last row without an \n here. We only need this \n in stdout in snapshot mode and when interactive,
405 // so piped snapshots don't have a weird newline in stdout that they don't need.
406 if (snapshot_mode && terminal_is_interactive()) {
407 printf("\n");
408 }
409 snapshot_done = true;
410 }
411 } else {
412 if (frames_rendered_since_first == 0) {
413 log_debug("SNAPSHOT: Waiting for first_frame_rendered (g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered=%d, g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered_ns=%llu)",
414 g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered, (unsigned long long)g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered_ns);
415 }
416 }
417 }
418
419 // Exit conditions: snapshot mode exits after capturing the final frame or initial paused frame
420 if (snapshot_mode && (snapshot_done || output_paused_frame)) {
421 log_info("SNAPSHOT: EXIT CONDITION MET - snapshot_done=%d, output_paused_frame=%d", snapshot_done, output_paused_frame);
422
423 // Calculate elapsed time from first frame captured (for video file)
424 // This is separate from render time - video should span from first capture to last capture
425 double capture_elapsed_sec = 0.0;
426 if (g_snapshot_first_capture_ns > 0) {
427 uint64_t now_ns = time_get_ns();
428 uint64_t elapsed_ns = now_ns - g_snapshot_first_capture_ns;
429 capture_elapsed_sec = (double)elapsed_ns / (double)NS_PER_SEC_INT;
430 log_info("SNAPSHOT: EXIT - Capture elapsed: %.3f seconds, render elapsed: %.3f seconds, g_snapshot_first_capture_ns=%llu",
431 capture_elapsed_sec,
432 (g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered_ns > 0) ? (double)(now_ns - g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered_ns) / NS_PER_SEC_INT : 0.0,
433 (unsigned long long)g_snapshot_first_capture_ns);
434 } else {
435 log_warn("SNAPSHOT: EXIT condition met but g_snapshot_first_capture_ns is 0!");
436 }
437
438 // Pass actual capture elapsed time to encoder for correct video duration
439 log_info("SNAPSHOT: About to call session_display_set_snapshot_actual_duration with duration=%.3f, display=%p",
440 capture_elapsed_sec, (void *)display);
441 if (display) {
442 session_display_set_snapshot_actual_duration(display, capture_elapsed_sec);
443 } else {
444 log_warn("SNAPSHOT: display context is NULL!");
445 }
446
447 // Signal application to exit in snapshot mode
448 APP_CALLBACK_VOID(signal_exit);
449 break;
450 }
451
452 // Frame rendering and timing details (moved down since snapshot exit is now above it)
453 if (ascii_frame) {
454
455 // Measure frame completion right after rendering, BEFORE keyboard polling
456 // This gives us accurate timing for just the core frame operations
457 uint64_t frame_end_render_ns = time_get_ns();
458
459 // Calculate each phase duration
460 uint64_t prestart_ms =
461 (capture_start_ns > frame_start_ns) ? (capture_start_ns - frame_start_ns) / NS_PER_MS_INT : 0;
462 uint64_t capture_ms =
463 (capture_end_ns > capture_start_ns) ? (capture_end_ns - capture_start_ns) / NS_PER_MS_INT : 0;
464 uint64_t convert_ms = conversion_elapsed_ns / NS_PER_MS_INT;
465 uint64_t render_ms =
466 (post_render_ns > pre_render_ns && post_render_ns > 0) ? (post_render_ns - pre_render_ns) / NS_PER_MS_INT : 0;
467 uint64_t total_ms =
468 (frame_end_render_ns > frame_start_ns) ? (frame_end_render_ns - frame_start_ns) / NS_PER_MS_INT : 0;
469
470 // Log phase breakdown every 5 frames
471 if (frame_count % 5 == 0) {
472 log_dev_every(
473 2 * NS_PER_SEC_INT,
474 "PHASE_BREAKDOWN[%lu]: prestart=%llu ms, capture=%llu ms, convert=%llu ms, render=%llu ms (total=%llu ms)",
475 frame_count, prestart_ms, capture_ms, convert_ms, render_ms, total_ms);
476 }
477 } else {
478 // Snapshot mode: even if frame conversion failed, check if we should exit
479 // This ensures snapshot_delay is honored even if display context isn't rendering
480 if (snapshot_mode && snapshot_done) {
481 break;
482 }
483 }
484
485 // In event-driven mode, frame rate limiting is handled by the sleep_cb callback
486 // Synchronous mode (with capture context) uses the pipeline and returns early above
487
488 // Note: Images returned by media sources are cached/reused and should NOT be destroyed
489 // The image pointers are managed by the source and will be cleaned up on source shutdown
490 } // while (!should_exit(user_data)) {
491
492 // Re-enable console logging after rendering completes
493 log_set_terminal_output(true);
494 if (!snapshot_mode && terminal_is_interactive()) {
495 printf("\n");
496 }
497
498 // Keyboard input cleanup (if it was initialized)
499 if (keyboard_enabled) {
500 keyboard_destroy();
501 log_debug_every(2 * NS_PER_SEC_INT, "Keyboard input disabled");
502 }
503
504 return ASCIICHAT_OK;
505}

◆ session_server_like_run()

asciichat_error_t session_server_like_run ( const session_server_like_config_t * config)

#include <src/common/session/server_like.h>

Run a server-like mode with unified initialization and shutdown

This function orchestrates the complete lifecycle of server-like modes by handling common boilerplate (keepawake, signal handlers) while delegating mode-specific logic to callbacks. Status screen threading and keyboard handling are the responsibility of individual modes.

Initialization Sequence

  1. Keepawake Validation & Setup:
    • Validates mutual exclusivity of –keepawake and –no-keepawake
    • Enables keepawake if requested (default: off for server modes)
  2. Signal Handler Registration:
    • Stores mode's interrupt_fn in static scope for signal context
    • Registers SIGINT and SIGTERM to call server_like_signal_wrapper()
    • Ignores SIGPIPE (TCP/WebSocket friendly)
    • interrupt_fn must be async-signal-safe and close listening sockets
  3. Mode-Specific Initialization:
    • Calls config->init_fn(config->init_user_data)
    • If init_fn fails, returns error code and proceeds to cleanup

Main Run Loop

Calls config->run_fn(config->run_user_data), which blocks until server shuts down. This is typically an accept loop (tcp_server_run, acds_server_run, etc.).

Cleanup (always runs, even on error)

  • Disables keepawake if it was enabled during init

Notes

  • Status screen threading is not managed by this layer. Modes that need status display should implement their own status_fn callback and thread management within run_fn.
  • The config->status_fn field is available for mode-specific use but is not invoked by server_like itself.
Parameters
configMode configuration. Must not be NULL. init_fn, run_fn, and interrupt_fn must not be NULL.
Returns
ASCIICHAT_OK on success, or the first error code from initialization or from run_fn. Cleanup always runs regardless of return value.

Definition at line 38 of file server_like.c.

38 {
39 if (!config) {
40 return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "config is NULL");
41 }
42
43 if (!config->init_fn) {
44 return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "init_fn is NULL");
45 }
46
47 if (!config->run_fn) {
48 return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "run_fn is NULL");
49 }
50
51 if (!config->interrupt_fn) {
52 return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "interrupt_fn is NULL");
53 }
54
55 asciichat_error_t result = ASCIICHAT_OK;
56
57 /* === INITIALIZATION === */
58
59 /* 1. Validate keepawake mutual exclusivity */
60 bool keepawake_enabled = GET_OPTION(enable_keepawake);
61 bool keepawake_disabled = GET_OPTION(disable_keepawake);
62 if (keepawake_enabled && keepawake_disabled) {
63 return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
64 "Cannot specify both --keepawake and --no-keepawake");
65 }
66
67 /* 2. Enable keepawake if requested (default is off for servers) */
68 if (keepawake_enabled) {
69 platform_enable_keepawake();
70 }
71
72 /* 3. Store interrupt callback for signal handlers */
73 g_server_like_interrupt_fn = config->interrupt_fn;
74
75 /* 4. Register signal handlers - note: signal handlers call interrupt_fn */
76 platform_signal(SIGINT, (signal_handler_t)server_like_signal_wrapper);
77 platform_signal(SIGTERM, (signal_handler_t)server_like_signal_wrapper);
78 platform_signal(SIGPIPE, SIG_IGN);
79
80 /* 5. Call mode-specific initialization */
81 result = config->init_fn(config->init_user_data);
82 if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
83 log_error("Mode initialization failed");
84 goto cleanup;
85 }
86
87 /* === RUN === */
88
89 /* 6. Call mode-specific blocking run loop */
90 result = config->run_fn(config->run_user_data);
91
92cleanup:
93 /* === CLEANUP === */
94
95 /* 7. Disable keepawake */
96 if (keepawake_enabled) {
97 platform_disable_keepawake();
98 }
99
100 return result;
101}

◆ session_settings_apply_to_options()

asciichat_error_t session_settings_apply_to_options ( const session_settings_t * settings)

#include <src/common/session/settings.h>

Apply settings to global options.

Parameters
settingsSettings to apply (must not be NULL)
Returns
ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure

Updates the global options system with values from the settings structure. Only modifies options that are represented in settings.

Definition at line 198 of file settings.c.

198 {
199 if (!settings) {
200 return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session_settings_apply_to_options: NULL settings");
201 }
202
203 // Update dimensions if specified
204 if (settings->width > 0 && settings->height > 0) {
205 asciichat_error_t width_result = options_set_int("width", (int)settings->width);
206 asciichat_error_t height_result = options_set_int("height", (int)settings->height);
207 if (width_result != ASCIICHAT_OK || height_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
208 log_warn("Failed to apply dimension settings: width=%d, height=%d", width_result, height_result);
209 }
210 }
211
212 // Note: Other options would require additional RCU update functions
213 // For now, dimensions are the primary use case for runtime updates
214 // Color mode, render mode, and palette typically don't change mid-session
215
216 return ASCIICHAT_OK;
217}

◆ session_settings_deserialize()

asciichat_error_t session_settings_deserialize ( const uint8_t * buffer,
size_t len,
session_settings_t * settings )

#include <src/common/session/settings.h>

Deserialize session settings from binary buffer.

Parameters
bufferInput buffer containing serialized settings
lenLength of input buffer in bytes
settingsOutput settings structure (must not be NULL)
Returns
ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure

Deserializes settings from binary format. Validates buffer length and extracts fields in network byte order.

Note
Returns ERROR_INVALID_PARAM if buffer is too small.

Definition at line 101 of file settings.c.

101 {
102 if (!buffer || !settings) {
103 return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session_settings_deserialize: NULL parameter");
104 }
105
106 // Check minimum buffer size
107 if (len < SESSION_SETTINGS_SERIALIZED_SIZE) {
108 return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session_settings_deserialize: buffer too small (%zu < %d)", len,
109 SESSION_SETTINGS_SERIALIZED_SIZE);
110 }
111
112 memset(settings, 0, sizeof(session_settings_t));
113 size_t offset = 0;
114
115 // Version (4 bytes, network byte order)
116 uint32_t version_net;
117 memcpy(&version_net, buffer + offset, sizeof(version_net));
118 settings->version = ntohl(version_net);
119 offset += sizeof(version_net);
120
121 // Width (2 bytes, network byte order)
122 uint16_t width_net;
123 memcpy(&width_net, buffer + offset, sizeof(width_net));
124 settings->width = (int16_t)ntohs(width_net);
125 offset += sizeof(width_net);
126
127 // Height (2 bytes, network byte order)
128 uint16_t height_net;
129 memcpy(&height_net, buffer + offset, sizeof(height_net));
130 settings->height = (int16_t)ntohs(height_net);
131 offset += sizeof(height_net);
132
133 // Color mode (1 byte)
134 settings->color_mode = buffer[offset++];
135
136 // Render mode (1 byte)
137 settings->render_mode = buffer[offset++];
138
139 // Palette type (1 byte)
140 settings->palette_type = buffer[offset++];
141
142 // Custom palette (32 bytes)
143 memcpy(settings->palette_custom, buffer + offset, 32);
144 settings->palette_custom[31] = '\0'; // Ensure null termination
145 offset += 32;
146
147 // Audio enabled (1 byte)
148 settings->audio_enabled = buffer[offset++];
149
150 // Encryption required (1 byte)
151 settings->encryption_required = buffer[offset++];
152
153 // Reserved (16 bytes)
154 memcpy(settings->reserved, buffer + offset, 16);
155 // offset += 16; // Unused, commented to avoid warning
156
157 return ASCIICHAT_OK;
158}

◆ session_settings_equal()

bool session_settings_equal ( const session_settings_t * a,
const session_settings_t * b )

#include <src/common/session/settings.h>

Compare two settings structures for equality.

Parameters
aFirst settings structure (must not be NULL)
bSecond settings structure (must not be NULL)
Returns
true if settings are equal (ignoring version), false otherwise

226 return false;
227 }
228
229 // Compare all fields except version

231 a->render_mode == b->render_mode && a->palette_type == b->palette_type &&
232 strncmp(a->palette_custom, b->palette_custom, sizeof(a->palette_custom)) == 0 &&
233 a->audio_enabled == b->audio_enabled && a->encryption_required == b->encryption_required;

◆ session_settings_from_options()

asciichat_error_t session_settings_from_options ( session_settings_t * settings)

#include <src/common/session/settings.h>

Populate settings from current global options.

Parameters
settingsOutput settings structure (must not be NULL)
Returns
ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure

Reads current values from the global options system and populates the settings structure. Version is set to current timestamp.

Definition at line 160 of file settings.c.

160 {
161 if (!settings) {
162 return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session_settings_from_options: NULL settings");
163 }
164
165 // Initialize to defaults
166 session_settings_init(settings);
167
168 // Get current options
169 const options_t *opts = options_get();
170 if (!opts) {
171 return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG, "Options not initialized");
172 }
173
174 // Set version to current time for ordering
175 settings->version = (uint32_t)time(NULL);
176
177 // Copy dimension settings
178 settings->width = (int16_t)opts->width;
179 settings->height = (int16_t)opts->height;
180
181 // Copy display settings
182 settings->color_mode = (uint8_t)opts->color_mode;
183 settings->render_mode = (uint8_t)opts->render_mode;
184 settings->palette_type = (uint8_t)opts->palette_type;
185
186 // Copy custom palette if set
187 if (opts->palette_custom_set && opts->palette_custom[0] != '\0') {
188 SAFE_STRNCPY(settings->palette_custom, opts->palette_custom, sizeof(settings->palette_custom));
189 }
190
191 // Copy audio/encryption settings
192 settings->audio_enabled = (uint8_t)opts->audio_enabled;
193 settings->encryption_required = opts->no_encrypt ? 0 : 1;
194
195 return ASCIICHAT_OK;
196}
options_get

◆ session_settings_init()

void session_settings_init ( session_settings_t * settings)

#include <src/common/session/settings.h>

Initialize session settings to defaults.

Parameters
settingsSettings structure to initialize (must not be NULL)

Initializes settings structure with sensible defaults:

  • Width/height: 0 (auto-detect)
  • Color mode: TERM_COLOR_AUTO
  • Render mode: RENDER_MODE_FOREGROUND
  • Palette: PALETTE_STANDARD
  • Audio: disabled
  • Encryption: required

Definition at line 26 of file settings.c.

26 {
27 if (!settings) {
28 return;
29 }
30
31 memset(settings, 0, sizeof(session_settings_t));
32
33 // Read all defaults from the RCU options system
34 settings->version = 0;
35 settings->width = (int16_t)terminal_get_effective_width();
36 settings->height = (int16_t)terminal_get_effective_height();
37 settings->color_mode = (uint8_t)GET_OPTION(color_mode);
38 settings->render_mode = (uint8_t)GET_OPTION(render_mode);
39 settings->palette_type = (uint8_t)GET_OPTION(palette_type);
40
41 // Custom palette
42 const char *palette = GET_OPTION(palette_custom);
43 if (palette && palette[0] != '\0') {
44 SAFE_STRNCPY(settings->palette_custom, palette, sizeof(settings->palette_custom));
45 }
46
47 settings->audio_enabled = (uint8_t)GET_OPTION(audio_enabled);
48 settings->encryption_required = GET_OPTION(no_encrypt) ? 0 : 1;
49}

◆ session_settings_needs_update()

bool session_settings_needs_update ( uint32_t local_version,
uint32_t remote_version )

#include <src/common/session/settings.h>

Check if settings need update based on versions.

Parameters
local_versionCurrent local settings version
remote_versionReceived remote settings version
Returns
true if local should update to remote, false otherwise

Determines if local settings should be updated based on version comparison. Higher version number wins (newer settings).

Definition at line 219 of file settings.c.

219 {
220 // Higher version wins (newer settings)
221 return remote_version > local_version;
222}

◆ session_settings_serialize()

asciichat_error_t session_settings_serialize ( const session_settings_t * settings,
uint8_t * buffer,
size_t * len )

#include <src/common/session/settings.h>

Serialize session settings to binary buffer.

Parameters
settingsSettings to serialize (must not be NULL)
bufferOutput buffer (must be at least SESSION_SETTINGS_SERIALIZED_SIZE bytes)
lenOutput parameter for actual serialized length (must not be NULL)
Returns
ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure

Serializes settings to a compact binary format suitable for network transmission. Uses network byte order for multi-byte integers.

Definition at line 51 of file settings.c.

51 {
52 if (!settings || !buffer || !len) {
53 return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session_settings_serialize: NULL parameter");
54 }
55
56 // Fixed-size serialization format
57 size_t offset = 0;
58
59 // Version (4 bytes, network byte order)
60 uint32_t version_net = htonl(settings->version);
61 memcpy(buffer + offset, &version_net, sizeof(version_net));
62 offset += sizeof(version_net);
63
64 // Width (2 bytes, network byte order)
65 uint16_t width_net = htons((uint16_t)(settings->width & 0xFFFF));
66 memcpy(buffer + offset, &width_net, sizeof(width_net));
67 offset += sizeof(width_net);
68
69 // Height (2 bytes, network byte order)
70 uint16_t height_net = htons((uint16_t)(settings->height & 0xFFFF));
71 memcpy(buffer + offset, &height_net, sizeof(height_net));
72 offset += sizeof(height_net);
73
74 // Color mode (1 byte)
75 buffer[offset++] = settings->color_mode;
76
77 // Render mode (1 byte)
78 buffer[offset++] = settings->render_mode;
79
80 // Palette type (1 byte)
81 buffer[offset++] = settings->palette_type;
82
83 // Custom palette (32 bytes, null-padded)
84 memcpy(buffer + offset, settings->palette_custom, 32);
85 offset += 32;
86
87 // Audio enabled (1 byte)
88 buffer[offset++] = settings->audio_enabled;
89
90 // Encryption required (1 byte)
91 buffer[offset++] = settings->encryption_required;
92
93 // Reserved (16 bytes)
94 memcpy(buffer + offset, settings->reserved, 16);
95 offset += 16;
96
97 *len = offset;
98 return ASCIICHAT_OK;
99}
